purpose: The overall report card: every major policy implemented 2014-2026 graded on delivered outcomes (matured cohort loop verdicts for the 2014-2020 era; disbursal report card for the PLI era; too-early-to-grade noted honestly for 2023-26 launches), plus the schemes open for investment right now and the ministries investors should approach first -- the program's one-page synthesis.
method: Synthesis of the matured-scheme cohort (18 launch-to-outcome traces, PRID-cited), the PLI disbursal report card (grades with stated criteria), the site-verified ministry catalog (open windows, portal quality), and the scheme registry (closures/attrition). Grades for the matured era are loop-based (did the incentive->investment->outcome loop close?); grades for the PLI era are delivery-based (disbursal + production). 2023-2026 launches are marked IN-FLIGHT, not graded -- the cohort shows the honest loop timescale is ~8-10 years.

A_closed_loops.outcome: 20% blending ~5y early; Rs 1.97L cr forex saved; ~316 lakh MT crude substituted
production 28x, exports 127x, imports 75% of demand -> 0.02%; smartphones = #1 export
A_closed_loops.design: capex subsidy + tariff protection -> production-linked -> brand incentives

A_closed_loops.outcome: LED price Rs ~310 -> Rs 38; 36.87cr bulbs; Rs 19,153cr/yr savings
57cr+ loans, Rs 40.07L cr disbursed (judged on disbursement; register silent on NPAs)
<500 -> 2.07 lakh recognised startups; FFS 1.0 fully deployed -> FoF 2.0
679 routes, 95 aerodromes, 1.68cr passengers; airports 74 -> 165; succeeded by Modified UDAN
15 rolling tranches; 100+ MT output; Rs 6,656cr/yr state revenue from tranche 1
Rs 5,433cr disbursed (36% of outlay); investment 237% of target; Penicillin-G restored; FDI +114%

B_partial_strong.outcome: 100.33 GW installed (3y late) but manufacturing loop closed: 2 -> 100 GW ALMM module capacity
the PLI family's other real payer; tenure completed, MPMS succeeds
port turnaround halved (96h -> 49.5h); EDFC complete; 1,002 rail cars exported -- execution loops still open
6 plants built (Matix, Ramagundam et al.); self-sufficiency missed -> NIPU-2026 extends the design

B_partial_strong.policy: PLI Food / Telecom / Bulk Drugs / MedDev
B_partial_strong.outcome: modest but real disbursal (Rs 1,084cr / ~1,175cr / ~55cr / 157cr); Food sunsets FY27

C_partial_weak.outcome: 29% of MW target after 7y; pumps work, grid-plants at 7% of sanctions
FDI $667bn decade cumulative but ~$81bn plateau since FY21; EoDB 142 -> 63
24 of 41 operational in 15 years; quietly discontinued 2021
Rs 281cr / Rs 0-by-design; White Goods beneficiary list slid 85 -> 80
Rs 30cr of Rs 120cr used; sector turnover ~7x; no successor

D_F_laggards.outcome: 50 of 5,000 plants (<1%); voluntary model abandoned for a mandate -- the register's largest miss
three redesigns; 2.15L GPs service-ready 12+ years in; usage metrics never published
Rs 2,378cr (accelerating) / Rs 48cr + 14-of-58 withdrawals / Rs 54.5cr to 2 unnamed firms
zero disbursed in five years; an awardee vanished; capacity re-tendered -- the family's F

decade_grades.in_flight_ungraded: 2023-2026 launches (Green Hydrogen SIGHT, critical-minerals chain, RDI fund, ELI, shipbuilding package, gasification 2026, BHAVYA, NIPU-2026, MPMS, Semicon 2.0) are IN-FLIGHT: the cohort's ~8-10-year loop timescale means grading them now would be premature -- their designs, however, are the proven family (guaranteed terms, procurement, capex).

open_for_investment_now.what: capital + interest + MSLI incentives; portal unnati.dpiit.gov.in
share of Rs 37,500cr; RFP on coal.gov.in

open_for_investment_now.window: DoP SPI: API-CF common facilities + SMDI calls
open_for_investment_now.what: capex support for pharma/medtech clusters, Schedule-M upgradation subsidy

open_for_investment_now.window: MoFPI: cold-chain EoI, CEFPPC units, PM-FME, Operation Greens claims

open_for_investment_now.what: market access for module/cell makers; DCR portal re-opened

open_for_investment_now.window: Startup stack (FoF 2.0 via AIFs, SISFS, CGSS) + CGTMSE/ZED
SATAT CBG EoI + mandatory blending demand
open_for_investment_now.what: assured-price offtake, now demand-backed by the blending mandate

tier_1_go_first.why: Best-maintained scheme section in government; live calls NOW; the PLI family's grade-A delivery record; parks + PRIP + SPI stack
A- delivery record; ECMS window extended, MPMS imminent, Semicon 2.0's six pillars incl. the new equipment/materials lane
The gateway itself (KYA -> single window, 32 ministries/34 states) + UNNATI deadline + BHAVYA parks + startup stack; owns the FDI rulebook
The transparency benchmark -- live application funnel, active loan portals, real subvention money
Gasification 2026 Round-1 open with a working auction machinery behind it (15 coal tranches prove execution)

tier_2_engage.why: Multiple open capex windows -- but PLISFPI sunsets FY27; enter via PMKSY components
ALMM window (31-Dec-2026), SIGHT CfPs; caveat: PLI payouts are post-commissioning by design
Rs 69,725cr package with fresh guidelines (Jan/May-2026); MDF still operationalising
Export instrument stack (RoDTEP/EPCG/EPM); NIRYAT PROTSAHAN new

tier_2_engage.ministry: MSME ministry (via portals, not the site)
tier_2_engage.why: CGTMSE Rs 10cr cover, ZED, RAMP -- use cgtmse.in / zed.msme.gov.in directly

approach_with_eyes_open.why: E-DRIVE claims deadlines near; ACC stalled at zero with stale pages; Capital Goods Ph-II proposal-based
24% Round-1 attrition disclosed; R1.2 MoU-stage; R&D window open
PLI windows amended three times in a year; verify on pli.texmin.gov.in (site is a JS shell; texmin.nic.in is dead)
NIPU-2026 and the critical-minerals chain are real money, but both departments' websites are dead/unreachable -- approach via Cabinet frameworks, NSWS and nodal PSUs

bottom_line: Twelve years of policy graded on outcomes: the A-row is uniformly demand-side guarantee/procurement designs, the F-row is voluntary uptake and unpaid production promises. For an investor entering now: the open money is capex/cluster/credit (not PLI), the reliable counterparties are the ministries with working portals and delivery records (DoP, MeitY, DPIIT/NSWS, FAHD, Coal), and the two dated deadlines that matter this quarter are UNNATI (30-Sep-2026) and ALMM List-II (31-Dec-2026).
